data.title: Stanley Black & Decker Reports Strong 1Q 2026 Results
data.description: The company is progressing well towards meeting its full-year targets for sales, margin, and cash, with the aerospace fasteners sale in the second quarter of 2026 generating approximately $1.6 billion in net proceeds. This transaction has enhanced the company's balance sheet and positioned it well for strategic capital investments. As a result of the anticipated gain in the second quarter related to CAM, the 2026 GAAP EPS guidance has been increased, while the 2026 adjusted EPS guidance remains unchanged. This positive development reflects the company's ongoing commitment to growth and financial performance in the aerospace industry.

data.title: Command Zero Accelerates SecOps Pipelines with APIs and MCP Server
data.description: Security Operations teams are now empowered to create customized tools and incorporate self-sufficient investigations into current processes with the introduction of new endpoints. On April 29, 2026, in Austin, Texas, Command Zero launched a comprehensive range of API endpoints and a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. This update provides teams with the ability to seamlessly integrate autonomous investigations.

data.title: Helena Capital Welcomes Katy Nelson as General Partner to Advance Its Next Phase of Growth
data.description: New York and Munich - Helena Capital has recently revealed that Katy Nelson has become the newest General Partner alongside existing General Partners Sarah Antor and Robin Wood Sailer. Nelson's appointment is a key development for the firm, as she brings extensive experience and strategic expertise to the team, ultimately contributing to shaping the firm's future direction. Prior to joining Helena Capital, Nelson spent five years at Andreessen Horowitz (a16z) as a Partner.

data.title: Ridge Security Brings Validated Attack Insights into CrowdStrike Falcon Next-Gen SIEM
data.description: In Silicon Valley, California, Ridge Security has revealed a new integration that allows automated penetration testing insights from RidgeBot® to seamlessly transfer into CrowdStrike Falcon® Next-Gen SIEM. With the rising number of vulnerabilities and alerts, security teams struggle to distinguish genuine exploitable risks. By incorporating RidgeBot's verified attack discoveries into the CrowdStrike Falcon® system, organizations can enhance their ability to prioritize remediation efforts effectively.

data.title: Lemonade Announces First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
data.description: Lemonade, Inc. (NYSE: LMND) has disclosed its financial performance for the first quarter of 2026 on its official website. Interested individuals can access the Q1 2026 Letter to Shareholders for detailed information. A conference call is scheduled for today at 8:00 am Eastern Time (5:00 am Pacific Time) to review the results. For those interested in participating, they can dial the toll-free numbers +1 (833) 461 5787 or +1 (585) 542 9983, using the access code: 708389519. Additionally, the live webcast can be accessed by visiting the Lemonade Inc. website.

data.title: Lookout Introduces Mobile AI Visibility and Governance to Expose Shadow AI Risk
data.description: Lookout, a leading mobile security company based in Boston, has expanded its mobile security platform by introducing Lookout AI Visibility & Governance. This new solution is tailored to offer organizations the necessary visibility to detect, manage, and safeguard AI implementation throughout their mobile infrastructure. By incorporating AI agent detection and policy management within the mobile space, Lookout fills a crucial visibility gap, empowering organizations to identify and secure their AI adoption.

data.title: Jozu Assessed "Awardable" for Department of Defense Work in the P1 Solutions Marketplace
data.description: In New York today, Jozu, a renowned company offering on-premises AI model registry and secure ML deployment technology, revealed their recognition as "Awardable" within the Platform One (P1) Solutions Marketplace. This marketplace serves as a digital hub showcasing succinct, post-competition pitch videos lasting 5 minutes or less, presenting solutions deemed notable for addressing vital government needs in hardware, software, and services. Jozu's advanced solutions aim to authenticate and safeguard data, ensuring top-notch security.

data.title: RunSafe Security Releases 2026 Medical Device Cybersecurity Index as Attacks Impacting Medical Devices Rise and Procurement Standards Tighten
data.description: A recent report, the 2026 Medical Device Cybersecurity Index released by RunSafe Security in McLean, Virginia, highlights concerning trends in cyberattacks on medical devices. The study found that a staggering 80% of such attacks result in disruptions to patient care, with 24% of healthcare organizations acknowledging incidents affecting their medical devices. The data, gathered through a survey of 551 healthcare professionals in the United States, United Kingdom, and Germany, reveals a pressing issue facing the industry. The findings underscore a significant challenge in balancing the need for enhanced procurement practices with the escalating threat landscape for medical devices.

data.title: Visa Moves Billions, Makes Billions — And Raises Outlook
data.description: Visa, Inc. (NYSE: V) saw a surge in premarket trading as the company unveiled its first-quarter results exceeding expectations. The earnings per share stood at $3.31, beating the projected $3.10, with revenue hitting $11.23 billion compared to the estimated $10.74 billion. Visa experienced a significant 17% year-over-year increase in net revenue, marking its speediest growth rate since 2022. Payments volume also rose by 9% to $3.7 trillion, while total transactions increased by 9% to 66 billion. The positive outcomes have led Visa to revise its full-year outlook upwards.

data.title: Jack Daniel's Maker Brown-Forman Stock Drops Nearly 6% In Pre-Market: What's Going On?
data.description: Brown-Forman (NYSE: BF) saw a 5.88% decrease in premarket trading as French spirits giant Pernod Ricard S.A. (OTC: PRNDY) and the Jack Daniel’s whiskey manufacturer mutually decided to end merger discussions. Pernod Ricard expressed confidence in its strategy and team, prioritizing long-term stakeholder value, while Brown-Forman stated its objectives to enhance global presence, fortify its brands, and streamline operations. Reports from the Wall Street Journal highlighted disagreements over the deal’s structure and financial terms, resulting in the inability of both parties to agree on merger conditions. Notably, both Pernod and Brown-Forman have notable family ownership, with any Brown-Forman deal necessitating approval from the majority-holding Brown family. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
